name: Swift Title Loans
description_short: Swift Title Loans Miami, Florida — Memphis car title lender offering $100–$50K loans with same-day funding for applicants with bad credit or past bankru...
description_long:
Swift Title Loans operates a single branch at 1473 S Trezevant St #23 in Memphis, Tennessee, offering secured lending against vehicle titles. The business is listed under the broader "Car Title Loans LA" network (cartitleloansla.com), indicating it is part of a multi-state title loan operation rather than an independent local lender. The Memphis location serves walk-in customers Monday through Friday 9am–7pm and Saturday 10am–5pm, with no Sunday hours.

The company offers car title loans split into two tiers: small emergency loans ($100–$2,000) and larger secured loans ($2,000–$50,000). The stated process is three steps: apply online, receive cash while retaining use of the vehicle, and repay the loan to reclaim the title. Same-day funding is available for applications completed by noon. The website explicitly welcomes applicants with bad credit and past bankruptcies, and provides a toll-free number (1-888-903-0217) and email (service@swiftloans.com) for direct contact. Online applications are secured with 256-bit SSL and TLS encryption.

Swift Title Loans positions itself on accessibility rather than cost transparency. The "keep your car" model differentiates it from pawnshop arrangements, allowing borrowers to continue using their vehicle during the loan term. The upper loan tier reaching $50,000 is notably higher than typical emergency title products, suggesting underwriting flexibility tied to vehicle equity. Extended weekday hours and Saturday availability make it more accessible than standard 9-to-5 lenders.

Several material cautions apply. The website provides no APR, fee, or rate information—a significant transparency gap given that title loans routinely carry annualized rates of 100–300% or more. The customer reviews on the page share near-identical phrasing, repeatedly reference the same staff member by name, and use phrases like "cloud-based personal loans" that suggest possible fabricated testimonials rather than organic feedback. Borrowers should independently verify terms before signing, as the loan terms disclosed at the branch may differ substantially from what the marketing implies.
pros: Explicitly accepts bad credit and past bankruptcies — no credit score minimum stated
Same-day cash disbursement available for applications completed before noon
Borrower keeps the vehicle during the loan term — title is returned upon repayment
Wide loan range from $100 up to $50,000 based on vehicle equity
Extended weekday hours (9am–7pm Mon–Fri) and Saturday availability (10am–5pm)
Online application with 256-bit SSL and TLS security
Toll-free phone and dedicated email for customer support
cons: No APR, interest rate, or fee information disclosed anywhere on the website
Single physical location serving Memphis, TN only — no broader geographic reach
Closed Sundays — limits same-day access for weekend emergencies
Customer reviews display highly repetitive language and identical references to a single staff member, raising authenticity concerns
Listing domain (cartitleloansla.com) suggests a franchise network, meaning loan terms and practices may vary by location without clear disclosure
best_for: Memphis-area residents who own a vehicle outright and need emergency cash within 24 hours
Borrowers with bad credit, no credit, or prior bankruptcy who have been rejected by banks and credit unions
Those needing larger secured amounts ($5K–$50K) against vehicle equity that payday lenders cannot cover
People who need access during extended weekday evening hours (up to 7pm)
diagnosis: Swift Title Loans is best suited for Memphis residents with vehicle equity who need emergency cash quickly and cannot qualify for conventional credit products. The primary caveat is a complete absence of rate or APR disclosure on the website — borrowers must obtain and carefully review the actual loan agreement before signing, as title loan costs are typically very high relative to other borrowing options.
services: Loans for applicants with prior bankruptcies
Vehicle-secured collateral loans with continued vehicle use
